title: Crossing Away From The Fermi Energy Is Not Yet A Clean Weyl Semimetal
summary: A topological crossing can exist in a band structure without controlling the low-energy physics; to identify a clean Weyl semimetal, the crossing must be relevant near the Fermi energy.
assumptions: Material or model filling is part of the physical question.
regime: Band structures with crossings that may or may not sit near the chemical potential.
scope: Lecture One distinction between formal crossings and low-energy semimetal phases.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One explicitly distinguishes a formal crossing from a physically active low-energy semimetal.

representation: Scope warning about physical relevance at the chosen filling.
failure_modes: Ignoring filling can lead to overstating the phase realized by a model or material.
retrieval_hints: Return this whenever a band crossing is discussed without specifying its location relative to the Fermi energy.
